instruction_set_metadata.purpose: CLI-ready implementation instructions to apply abstraction-governance changes across the newPhasePlanProcess v3.2 files, update schema support, add validators, and wire new gates.
instruction_set_metadata.target_outcome: Upgrade the planning system so abstraction is mechanically captured and validated during planning rather than informally implied.

must_preserve_existing_behavior: Existing v3.2 planning sections and semantics unless explicitly extended by this instruction set
Existing gate registry entries unless explicitly amended
Existing pattern-first and no-implied-behavior rules unless strengthened

preconditions: All target files must exist before mutation begins
A baseline backup or git snapshot must be captured before any writes
JSON must be parsed and re-serialized canonically after each modification batch
Instruction document content must be transformed structurally, not by naive blind text append

execution_plan.title: Load and validate source artifacts
actions: Verify each required file exists
Parse JSON files
Verify cleaned_final_abstraction_patch_plan.json contains strict_rfc6902_object_pointer_operations and companion_array_resolution_instructions

execution_plan.title: Create backups and baseline snapshot
actions: Create timestamped backup copies of all target files
Compute SHA256 hashes of each original file
Record current git branch and clean/dirty status if repository exists

execution_plan.title: Patch template object-pointer sections
actions: Apply only strict RFC-6902 object-pointer operations for the template file
Do not apply array operations in this step
Abort if any object-pointer path is missing unless path creation is explicitly part of op semantics

execution_plan.title: Resolve template array instructions and convert to concrete RFC-6902 ops
actions: Resolve validation_gates entries by gate_id
Resolve ai_instructions arrays by exact field name
Resolve any other mutable arrays using the companion selector instructions
Generate a concrete RFC-6902 ops file before applying

execution_plan.title: Add step-level abstraction_contract support in template step contracts
actions: Extend the canonical shape of step contracts so executable steps can include abstraction_contract
Do not attempt to populate every existing concrete step instance with values unless the template already uses reusable defaults
Ensure the template communicates that abstraction_contract is required when steps depend on abstracted paths, identities, executors, interfaces, or artifacts

execution_plan.title: Patch technical specification object-pointer sections
actions: Apply strict object-pointer operations only
Preserve existing documentation structure
Update version metadata to 3.3.0

execution_plan.title: Transform instruction document content
actions: Update document_metadata.version to 3.3.0
Parse the markdown content string into section-aware segments
Apply the structured section-aware transform instructions from the patch plan
Rebuild the full markdown content string
Write the updated JSON document back out
validation_rules: Do not remove existing mandatory sections
Ensure new abstraction sections appear in the section-by-section instructions
Ensure new general principles bullets are present exactly once

execution_plan.title: Upgrade plan schema for abstraction sections
actions: Add new top-level properties: abstraction_governance, abstraction_registry, abstraction_decision_rules, shared_core_allocation, resolver_contracts
Add these new sections to the schema required list if the template treats them as mandatory
Define nested schemas for each new top-level section
Extend step contract schema to support abstraction_contract
Extend any planning_hierarchy item schemas to allow implementation_abstraction
Extend execution_patterns schema to allow abstraction_contract metadata
Extend traceability schema to support abstraction mappings

files_to_create.purpose: Validate plan-wide abstraction policy and required policy fields
Validate declared abstracted resources and required fields
Validate resolver definitions and fail-closed behavior
Ensure every abstraction reference resolves through a declared resolver
Detect forbidden direct references such as cross-module literal paths
Detect duplicated shared responsibilities across CLIs/modules/workstreams
Detect unstable identity usage for rename/move/reorder-sensitive entities

execution_plan.title: Define validator implementation contract
actions: All validators must parse input JSON safely
All validators must emit a structured JSON result with validator_id, passed, findings, evidence_paths, and failure_codes
All validators must support deterministic ordering of findings
All validators must fail closed on malformed input
All validators must avoid implicit defaults for missing critical fields

execution_plan.title: Wire new gates into template and spec
actions: Ensure GATE-ABS-001 through GATE-ABS-005 exist in the template validation_gates collection
Ensure matching gate registry entries exist in the technical specification
Add these gates to the execution boundary or required validation flow where abstraction correctness must be proven before execution
Preserve existing gate sequencing and make abstraction gates pre-execution gates

execution_plan.title: Update any gate dependency graph or orchestration index
actions: Search for gate dependency files, gate ordering files, or plan CLI gate registries that must include the new abstraction gates
If such files exist, update them so the new gates are runnable and ordered before execution
If no dependency file exists, record that fact in evidence and stop without inventing one
search_targets: other gate registry or orchestration files referenced by the technical specification

execution_plan.title: Run schema validation against modified template
actions: Validate NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_AUTONOMOUS_DELIVERY_TEMPLATE_V3_2.json against the upgraded schema
Validate NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION_V3_2.json is still valid JSON and internally coherent
Validate NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_INSTRUCTION_DOCUMENT_V3_2.json is valid JSON and content string is non-empty

execution_plan.title: Create golden positive fixture
actions: Create a minimal example plan instance that uses abstraction_governance
Include one abstracted path resource
Include one resolver contract
Include one shared-core allocation entry
Include one step-level abstraction_contract
Run all new abstraction validators against it

execution_plan.title: Create negative fixtures
actions: Create one fixture with cross-module literal path
Create one fixture with missing resolver
Create one fixture with duplicated shared service logic
Create one fixture with unstable identity strategy
Create one fixture with a step that uses abstraction but omits abstraction_contract

execution_plan.title: Execute validator suite against fixtures
actions: Run each new validator against the positive fixture and require pass
Run each relevant validator against negative fixtures and require fail where intended
Emit a summary report

execution_plan.title: Finalize and emit modification report
actions: Compute post-modification hashes for all changed files
Diff original vs modified target files
Emit a final report of created and modified files
List any unresolved follow-up items such as additional orchestration files that need future work

template_specific_rules: Do not hardcode array indexes when inserting new gates
Use gate_id matching for validation_gates resolution
If ai_instructions structure differs from assumed shape, inspect actual object and adapt using same semantic intent, then record adaptation in evidence
technical_spec_specific_rules: Use exact string matching or stable object selectors for key_capabilities, design_principles, required_sections, gate_registry, and glossary terms
Do not duplicate existing entries if semantically equivalent text is already present
instruction_document_specific_rules: Because /content is markdown text inside JSON, treat all edits as structured markdown transforms then replace the full string once
Do not insert the same abstraction section twice
Preserve existing formatting style where possible

gate_contracts.name: Resolver Completeness
must_fail_if: resolver points to missing source of truth

gate_contracts.name: Direct Reference Violation Audit
must_fail_if: cross-module literal path is used where abstraction is required
identity depends on mutable index or direct unstable locator

gate_contracts.name: Shared Core Duplication Audit
must_fail_if: same shared responsibility appears in multiple components without declared shared_core_allocation or exception

gate_contracts.name: Identity Stability Audit
must_fail_if: rename/move/reordering-sensitive entities lack stable identity abstraction
